title: Pluchea balsamifera
taxonomy: Pluchea balsamifera is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pluchea, which consists of shrubs and herbs found in various tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is native to parts of Asia and Africa, and its classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Magnoliophyta, class - Magnoliopsida, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Pluchea, species - P. balsamifera. The classification indicates its botanical name and its relationships to other plants within the plant kingdom. This classification system is based on the plant's characteristics, growth habits, and evolutionary history.
uses: Pluchea balsamifera is a plant with various medicinal uses, including treating respiratory issues such as colds and coughs. It is also used to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat skin conditions like eczema and acne. Pluchea balsamifera is believed to possess antibacterial properties, which may help prevent infections. Additionally, it is sometimes used as a natural remedy for fever reduction.
benefits: Pluchea balsamifera has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and roots are rich in essential oils, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to help in reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and soothing respiratory issues. Its antiseptic and antifungal properties make it effective against skin infections and wounds. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits and potential applications.

constituents: Pluchea balsamifera contains active constituents including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
